description:
Low odor: Mild odor. Slightly Fresh Solvent character Fruity Citrusy Green

Soluble in water yet high solubility with fragrance compounds too.
Unique volatility properties making it suitable for air freshener applications with up to about 30% in the finished product with other solvents, alcohol, glycols etc. 
Helps adjust and stabilize the perfume evaporation rate and minimize changes during evaporation.
In surfactant water-based products helps reduce the amount of solubiliser required.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of 3-Methyl-3-Methoxy Butanol (MMB) 

                                          
                                          
                                          Low odor: Mild odor Slighly fruity, citrus. green
High compatibility with fragrance compounds: Provides high compatibility with a broad range of fragrance compounds
Useful in air fresheners up to about 30% in the finished product and blended  with other solvents, glycols, ethanol, water. Unique volatility properties: Ability to adjust the aromatic evaporation rate and minimize composition changes during evaporation
Surfactant products: Enables reduced use of surfactants in water-based aromatics 
Mixed with water over 20%, there is no flash point

synonyms: $AN : A-ALDEHYDE F-TEC™ is a primary odour perfume compound base, a mixture of aroma chemicals, isolates and essential oils for use in perfume compounding. Great for quickly adding special effects, rapid prototyping and perfume creation with easier IFRA & EU compliance. When combined with other F-TEC bases, as in the F-TEC Custom Creation Kit, it forms a powerful super-fast and flexible system of perfume creation for most fine perfumery and toiletry product applications. description:
Agarwood Oil Siam is produced by a distiller and graduate of PerfumersWorld that we have worked with for over 10 years. This specific oil is our favorite and guaranteed natural from plantations close to the Thai-Cambodian border and produced by ethical and sustainable methods.
Agarwood Oil Siam has the typical agarwood/oud character with, rich woody balsamic, animalic-phenolic and leather notes, only slightly woody-smoky. It does NOT have the undesirable green-wood notes of trees that have been harvested too young.

description: Agarwood Siam 10% in Nargamotha  This is an ALL NATURAL BLEND of the finest Siam Agarwood Oil with Indian Nargamotha that gives a lot of the power and richness of Agarwood at a fraction of the price and without resorting to any of the artificial "oud" molecules found in most mass marketed fragrances. The blend is suitable for the finest haute couture perfumery, natural perfumery and aromatherapy. Indispensible in Oriental, Chypre, Fougere, Russian Leather, Suede, Exotic Wood, Tobacco, and Forest fragrances. Whilst perceived primarily as a "masculine" note it should not be overlooked for its trace usage (below 0.1%) in the background of fine women's fragrances for its depth and sillage boosting effect..

description:
The Ozonic marine base from Synarome in the 1950s (pre-Calone) begins with a delicate rosy accent surrounded by a marine cascade of lily symphony. The background is reminiscent of sand and shells.
It gives a natural aqueous facet to compositions and increases the green facet of jasmine, gardenia, narcissus or lily of the valley perfumes. It blends well with Chypre notes to induce modernity.

description: (Reported as a Male pheromone primarily attractive to females, gay-partners) *Not available in Thailand
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Alpha-Male Concentrate

                                          
                                          
                                          Using Androstenone 1000 ppm Concentrate
We recommended to using Androstenone 1000 ppm in fragrance compounds at a maximum of 0.01 to 0.1% (w/w) 0.1 to 1 gram per Kg or Litre of perfume compound. This will ensure that the active Androstenone content does not exceed 1 ppm 1 part-per-million which has been found to be the upper limit at which the pheromones are esthetically acceptable in odour.
The materials used are high purity reconstructions using synthetic sources and no human or animal extracts were used in their production.
Depending upon the subject, it is reported to be an unpleasant, sweaty, urinous smell, intensely masculine, a sandalwood-like smell, or even a pleasant floral smell.
Is pheromone found in both male and female sweat and urine. It is also found in boar's saliva, and in celery cytoplasm. Androstenone was the first mammalian pheromone to be identified. It is found in high concentrations in the saliva of male pigs, and, when inhaled by a female pig that is in heat, results in the female assuming the mating stance. Androstenone is the active ingredient in 'Boarmate', a commercial product sold to pig farmers.
In small amounts, the odor is hardly detectable by most people. This may be due to a polymorphism in the receptor gene that codes for the androstenone receptor. However, the ability to detect the odor varies greatly. It has been shown that the odor can be detected by people down to levels of 0.2 parts per billion to 0.2 parts in 100 million. Several groups report, however, that some individuals who initially cannot smell androstenone can learn to smell it by repeated exposures to it.  
To animals, the smell of androstenone can act as a social sign of dominance, or it can be a way of attracting a mate. This smell, to some animals, has a huge impact on behavioral patterns in the specimen. It is unproven that humans are affected in the same way.
Source Wikipedia.

odour_raw:
The odour and uses of AMBERGRIS F-TEC 

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=>It has a distinct, animalic odor that is often described as earthy, marine, or musky. Some describe the smell as being similar to that of a damp forest floor, while others say it has a sweet, mossy aroma. 
Others detect notes of vanilla, tobacco, or even a slight fecal odor.

 Synaesthesia=>beige, crumbly, waxy, silky smooth, cigar, oboe, white leather, ash 

 Perfume-Uses=>blender, fixative, tobacco, chypre, fougere, leather, wood, cedar, oudh, agarwood 

 Tips=> Use with iso E Super, Cedramber, Amber, Methyl ionone, Oud

 Just-try-it=>Vanilla

description: Ambernoir F-TEC is a succulent material for those wishing to work with a classic “dirtier”, more animalic ambergris material as opposed to many of the modern clean or clean-linen types.
odour_raw: The odour and uses of AMBERNOIR F-TEC | Odour=>Ambernoir F-TEC has a powerful, rich, and deep character with marked ambergris, leather, castoreum, and balsamic notes. It is fecal and musty with a woody amber and labdanum dimension. Ambernoir F-TEC represents the animalic character of natural Ambergris, missing from many modern chemical substitutes. Other descriptors: ambergris, old, wood, musty, cistus, fecal, animal, labdanum, amber, animal, clean dry earth, humus, fresh & clean, incense, musk, woody, salty, oceanic | Synaesthesia=> Submerged in dark, deep, salty sea. Prehistoric, Cambrian, whale with ribbed jaw, masculine, strong, powerful | Perfume-Uses=> Useful in reproductions of classic style fragrances in the families of Oriental, Chypre, Leather, Suede, and Fougere. Ambernoir F-TEC is created from over 90% natural extracts; It does not contain any animal sourced products.

description:
Ambrettolide is a rich but delicate animallic musk used in traces (from 0.01%) in all types where it adds diffusion warmth roundness. If overused it may suppress top notes. An excellent blender-fixative. 
Note: many are anosmic to, i.e. unable to smell, ambrettolide.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Ambrettolide

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=>  natural-musk ambrette-seed rich and extremely tenacious floral-musky sweet odour. Ambrettolide is used at 0.01% or less in alcoholic solutions to help soften the alcohol odour leaving a faint floral-musky sweet and pleasant odour.

description:
Ambrofix from Givaudan. From their description Ambrofix is a highly powerful, highly substantive and highly stable ambery note for use in all applications. The most suitable material for obtaining an authentic ambergris note. Produced from a natural starting material, this chiral product contains one main high impact isomer. Effective from the smallest traces, through to 2% or even more and lasts for over a month on a smelling strip. One of the most useful chemicals in modern perfumery.

(Formerly SKU : 1UW14382)
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Ambrofix

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=> ambergris old paper sweet labdanum dry: Incense, Oriental strong woody-amber, sweet-earth, musky odor with a delicate animal tonality use: for amber notes in fragrances and as a fixative. Ambery, Woody, Tobacco, Dry Use: Ambrofix is a highly powerful,highly substantive and highly stable ambery note for use in all applications. Ambrofix is the most suitable material for obtaining an authentic ambergris note. Produced from a natural starting material, this chiral product contains one main high impact isomer. Typical Ambergris note strongly amber, woody and dry notes. Flavor: balsamic amber-like, woody and fresh with a piney and nutty notes. Strong Woody-Amber, Musky Use: Used in Flavour and Fragrance industry. extremely powerful and elegant amber note with exceptional substantivity, typical ambergris note Use: Stable in: body lotion (very good), shampoo (very good), soap (good), ap roll-on (good), powder (very good), cleaner citric (good), cleaner apc (very good), bleach (very good). Flavor: woody, cedar wood, floral, lea

description:
Ambrox Super by Firmenich (it is the refined ORIGINAL AMBROXAN created by Firmenich but when they lost control of that trademark after their patent expired they had to rename it Ambrox and Ambrox Super.) The materials now called Ambroxan are the generic versions. 
 AMBROX® SUPER has an extremely powerful and elegant ambergris odor with a musky, woody tonality. It is highly diffusive and brings a unique ambery effect from top notes to dry down.
Note: Ambrox Super is a white powdered solid so you may find that the 10% solution in DPG we offer is more suitable for you if you don't have access to a scale.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Ambrox Super

                                          
                                          
                                          Extremely powerful and elegant ambery odor with musky and woody tonalities.
Sparklingly sensual, AMBROX® SUPER offers musky and woody tonalities to amber ingredients. Compared to CETALOX®, this profile is more natural, animalic, and closer to the original ambregris.

PERFUMERY USAGE
AMBROX® SUPER is highly diffusive and brings a unique ambery effect from top notes to dry down. It delivers all the promises of sleek elegance and powerful iconic ambery signature.

description:
Apricot Oil is a bland oily carrier oil. It has slippery texture with slow absorption into the skin. Ideal for mixing with other carrier oils (5-10%) to increase slip to aid prolonged massage without becoming too sticky.

Not really recommended as a solvent in perfumery compounds due to poor solubility of resins, citrus oils (terpenes), powders and crystals plus will tend to produce rancid notes over time.

description:
Avocado Oil is a carrier oil used in massage oil blends. It has an oily texture with a quite slow absorption rate into the skin. Add a just enough to a too-fast absorbing blend (e.g 5-10%) to give you the desired absorption rate. 
Not really recommended as a solvent in perfumery compounds due to poor solubility of resins, citrus oils (terpenes), powders and crystals plus will tend to produce rancid notes.

The odour and uses of Avocado Oil - Fixed Oil

                                          
                                          
                                          Bland oily note used as a carrier oil for essential oils or as a massage oil. Oily texture and quite slow absorbtion into the skin.

description:
Azarbre has a a Fresh cool dry Woody Iris-like character with a medicinal honey note and aspects of dried flowers and the fresh notes of Patchouli. 
Keywords: Iris, Orris, Woody fragrances, Gourmand, Herbal, Cooling, modifier for Ionones, Methyl Ionones, Patchouli, Clearwood, Vertenex, or Verdox,. 
(former SKU was 4WB15147)
odour_raw:

                                          Odour=> dry woody chrysanthemum phenolic powdery orris: Honey, Woody, Warm, Orris. Use: Azarbre has a warm honey note with aspects of dried flowers. description: A milky-smooth banana
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of BANANA F-TEC

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=> banana, fruity, creamy with Spice, Vanilla, Musk, Wood, Balsam, Muguet, Dairy, Green notes

 Synaesthesia=>banana cake, cool banana smoothie, still life painting of a fruit bowl, Just Yellow, Banana

 Perfume-Uses=> Fruity notes where some non-dairy creaminess is required. Especially ripe tropical fruits like Mango, Jackfruit, Durian, Papaya or Sweet Corn where sulfur notes are present.

  Tips=> 
A modifier for Narcotic Florals and Gourmand family perfumes. Fruit salad. 
 Just-try-it=> 1% in a Jasmine composition or add a trace to green notes for novelty

description: Benzophenone is sweet, aromatic with a faint rose and fruity character
odour_raw: The odour and uses of Benzophenone | Odour=>Benzophene has a faint and fresh characteristic with touches of fruits and florals. It has a very faint, powdery-rosy geranium-like odor with a slightly metallic note. Other descriptors: vinyl faint powdery-rosy. On skin by itself, Benzophene will become woodier on the dry down, like a mellow wood that has been lacquered (however, not in an unpleasant way). synonyms: $CL : Bergamot Fleuressence™ is a primary odour perfume compound base, a mixture of aroma chemicals, isolates and essential oils for use in perfume compounding. Used with other aroma materials and essential oils it is great for quickly adding special effects, rapid prototyping and perfume creation. When combined with other Fleuressence bases, as in the Fleuressence Custom Creation Kit, it forms a part of a powerful super-fast and flexible system of perfume creation for most fine perfumery applications. Q. What are Fleuressence and F-TEC Bases?  "Fleuressence vs F-TEC"

description: Birch tar Oil is a powerful and smoky, high-impact oil that smells of burnt wood, tar, and oil
odour_raw: The odour and uses of Birch Tar Oil Rectified | Odour=>Birch Tar Oil Rectified is perhaps the sharpest of the three main tar oils (including Cade and Pine oil). It has a powerful smoky tar/burnt wood aroma, as if a fire is lightly smoldering. It is dense and gummy. When smelled in its raw form, it is sharp and can remind one of petrol. It has a similar profile to Cade Oil. Other descriptors: phenolic, burnt, leathery, medicinal, aromatic, slightly spicy, burnt wood, coal tar, smoked meat or fish, and Wrights Coal Tar Soap | Synaesthesia=> fumes, bonfire, hillbilly, smokey cloud, campfire, charred wood, smoldering, bar fight, red neck in a truck with elbow and beer dangling from the window, cigar | Perfume-Uses=> Fantasy Blends; Leather, Russian Leather, Cuir Woody | Blends Well With=> +Cananga +Isoamyl Phenyl Acetate +Heptyl Eugenol +Guaiyl Butyrate | Tips=> As with most oils from the Tar and Smoke family, it is essential to dilute them to your specific needs as they can instantly overpower a fragrance
synaesthesia: fumes, bonfire, hillbilly, smokey cloud, campfire, charred wood, smoldering, bar fight, red neck in a truck with elbow and beer dangling from the window, cigar
perfume_uses: Fantasy Blends; Leather, Russian Leather, Cuir Woody
tips: As with most oils from the Tar and Smoke family, it is essential to dilute them to your specific needs as they can instantly overpower a fragrance

odour: Birch Tar Oil Rectified is perhaps the sharpest of the three main tar oils (including Cade and Pine oil). It has a powerful smoky tar/burnt wood aroma, as if a fire is lightly smoldering. It is dense and gummy. When smelled in its raw form, it is sharp and can remind one of petrol. It has a similar profile to Cade Oil. Other descriptors: phenolic, burnt, leathery, medicinal, aromatic, slightly spicy, burnt wood, coal tar, smoked meat or fish, and Wrights Coal Tar Soap

description: Black Tea Base is a complex blend derived from natural black tea leaves with a few added compounds.
odour_raw: The odour and uses of Black Tea Base | Odour=>Black Tea Base opens with bitter black tea leaves and a gentle, brief wine aroma, but then settles into a complex, faintly sweeter version with a tasty, dry herb tea aroma; well-rounded with many facets. The brief wine aroma at the beginning with a tinge of fruitiness is very faint but noticeable—think of it as a faint watercolor in the background, while the center is a large dark bowl of dry and shriveled black tea leaves. This base develops pleasantly over time. | Synaesthesia=> tied block of fresh black tea leaves, tasty, bottle of wine, a watercolor painting made with wine and tea leaf, the sound of chopping herbs on a wooden cutting board, dry, shriveled black leaves | Perfume-Uses=>as a perfume modifier, smooth herb, | Just-try-it=>This compound has many facets, including aromas of gentle wine, sweet tobacco leaves, faint black freshly-ground pepper, spices, hint of sweet lavender, sweet clove | Tips=>

description: powerful blackcurrant minty berry fruity green blackberry jammy incense buchu
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Blackcurrant Absolute

                                          
                                          
                                          
fruity and also has green minty-sulfurous and animalic notes, giving it a lasting, sparkling character
Blackcurrant is a bushy perennial shrub that grows one to two meters high. Its botanical name is Ribes nigrum L. Native to the colder regions of the Northern Hemisphere, blackcurrant is now mainly grown in Poland and England, along with the Burgundy and Loire Valley regions of France. The large, bright-green leaves are divided into five lobes with finely serrated tips. The underside of each leaf is covered with odoriferous glands containing the potent, fruity fragrance characteristic of blackcurrant. In springtime, the plant becomes covered with small green flowers forming hanging clusters, which give way to juicy, black berries to be harvested in summer. Blackcurrant grows wild in cool regions, as the plant’s buds need cold enough temperatures to properly develop. The leaf buds are what are used to produce our 100% pure and natural French blackcurrant bud absolute. These very fragrant parts are harvested in January and February and processed into concrete by extraction with volatile solvents. The absolute is then obtained by ethanol extraction of the concrete. Just as with cognac or davana, it exudes a fruity fragrance and also has sulfurous and animalic notes, giving it a lasting, sparkling character.

odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Bonding Concentrate

                                          
                                          
                                          Using Bonding Concentrate
Evokes feelings of contentment, reductions in anxiety, and feelings of calmness and security when in the company of the mate.
We recommended to using Bonding concentrate 0.01% in fragrance compounds at a maximum of 0.01 to 0.1% (w/w) 0.1 to 1.0 grams per Kg or Litre of perfume compound. This will ensure that the active content does not exceed 1 ppm 1 part-per-million which has been found to be the upper limit at which the odour is esthetically acceptable.

description: Bourgeonal has a fresh floral watery muguet note lilial like aldehydic character and a woody  sandalwood depth reminiscent of notes deep in  rive-gauche and calandre
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Bourgeonal

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=>  Bourgeonal has a fresh floral watery muguet note lilial like aldehydic character and a woody sandalwood depth. Reported to have Pheromone like properties with males tending to be more sensitive to it and attracted to it. 
Perfume-Uses=>  Floral fragrances especially Muguet, Rose aldehydic compositions like Rive-Gauche, Calandre,
 
Occurs-in=> Muguet, The odor of lily of the valley, specifically the ligand Bourgeonal, was thought to attract mammalian sperm. The 2003 discovery of this phenomenon prompted research into odor reception, but a 2012 study demonstrated instead that at high concentrations, Bourgeonal imitated the role of progesterone in stimulating sperm to swim (chemotaxis), a process unrelated to odor reception. (Wikipedia)
 Muguet notes, Rose, Aldehydes, Sandalwood

description: Butylated Hydroxytoluene (BHT) is probably the most efficient anti-oxidant used in perfumery. It is almost odourless in use but as a pure white to off-white crystalline powder has a very faint musty cresylic phenolic odour. Used from 0.1% in citrus oils, aliphatic aldehydes, fixed oils and many other oxygen sensitive materials, compounds and finished products it can greatly extend their shelf and odour life and also slow down, but not completely stop, colour changes.

synonyms: $BH : CAMPHOR F-TEC™ is a primary odour perfume compound base, a mixture of aroma chemicals, isolates and essential oils for use in perfume compounding. Green, Wine, Brandy, Slightly Buttery Green, fruity, brandy-like odor with butter undertone. | Just-try-it=>Other suggested uses: to evoke the following aromas or enhance them : Apple, Citrus Fruits, Dairy Products, Hard Fruits, Mango, Pineapple, Raspberry, Soft Fruits, Tea wine-like, green, cognac or brandy, slightly buttery : butter; green; wine-like | Tips=> Note: not as pungent or as sharp as some green cis-3 chemicals

description: Citric Acid  cyrystals are almost odourless with a sharp lemon-fruity-sour taste. Used as a chelating agent to remove Fe (iron) from oils and compounds. Fe contamination comes from storage in steel drums or during distillation causing severe darkening of the oil. Oils commonly affected by Fe contamination patchouli clove cinnamon vetiver lemongrass litsea cubeba lemon orange grapefruit. Add a pinch (or <1%) of citric acid to a material and wait 7-14 days to see if it lightens. The crystals precipitate so just pour off (decant) the oil from the top or filter the crystals out through a course filter paper.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Citric Acid

                                          
                                          
                                          Virtually odorless, used as a chelating agent to remove Fe (iron contamination from steel drums or during distillation), It has an intense sour fruity lemon taste. 
Useful for lightening Patchouli Oil, Clove Oil, Cinnamon Oil, Vetivert Oil if they darken after storage in steel drums. Add about 0.5-1% w/w to the oil and leave for about 7-14 days. the citric acid can be removed by decanting the clear liquid off the top or filtering through a fine mesh.
It is used in many flavours for its sourness such as Lemon, Orange, Grapefruit, Lime, Berry, Fruit-flavour, Food-additive, Candy, Chewing-gum, Jello, most fruit flavors to add tartness.
synonyms: $ZX : beta-Hydroxy tricarballylic acid : 2-Hydroxy-1,2,3-propanetricarboxylic acid : Citric acid :

description: A Carnation and Orchid spicy mélange of balsams, jasmine, and orchid with slight woodiness, less citrus than the Fleuressence version. A timeless old spice “structural framework” which can be used as the heart of a masculine, feminine or non-binary perfume depending on which modifiers are used.
odour_raw: The odour and uses of CLASSIC SPICE F-TEC | Odour=> A Carnation and Orchid spicy mélange of balsams, jasmine, and orchid with slight woodiness, less citrus than the Fleuressence version. A timeless old spice “structural framework” which can be used as the heart of a masculine, feminine or non-binary perfume depending on which modifiers are used. | Synaesthesia=> Reddy-brown, Tan, Carnation and Orchid flower bouquet, Classic old spice note, “Reminds me of my Dad when I was growing up”, Mad Men advertising executives from the 50’s and 60’s, Comforting, Secure, Stable, Old Spice, L’air du Temps, Fidji, Saxaphone, The Blues, Cosy, Oak and light coloured woods, Classy, | Perfume-Uses=> A ready made neutral Spicy Floral framework upon which countless variations are possible. Adding Forals, Vanilla, Gourmand notes will tend to move more towards a feminine theme and Spices, Citrus, Herbs, Pine, Woods, Animalic, Leather, Tobacco notes will slide it towards classic masculine tastes. | Tips=>Use 5-10% as a blender when a fragrance seems to be all over the place with too many things going on. | Just-try-it=>With single spice essential oils. For example with 20% Black Pepper Oil, or 5% Cinamon Leaf Oil
synaesthesia: Reddy-brown, Tan, Carnation and Orchid flower bouquet, Classic old spice note, “Reminds me of my Dad when I was growing up”, Mad Men advertising executives from the 50’s and 60’s, Comforting, Secure, Stable, Old Spice, L’air du Temps, Fidji, Saxaphone, The Blues, Cosy, Oak and light coloured woods, Classy,
perfume_uses: A ready made neutral Spicy Floral framework upon which countless variations are possible. Adding Forals, Vanilla, Gourmand notes will tend to move more towards a feminine theme and Spices, Citrus, Herbs, Pine, Woods, Animalic, Leather, Tobacco notes will slide it towards classic masculine tastes.
tips: Use 5-10% as a blender when a fragrance seems to be all over the place with too many things going on.

The odour and uses of Cocoa Absolute

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=>  cocoa cocoa bitter powdery milky : roasted : Pyrogenic/cocoa bitter, powdery, milky, liqueur like
: coffee 
Use: The powder of the cocoa bean serves as a raw material for a range of Cocoa extracts. The cacao tree, which grows in most tropical countries, develops fruits, more commonly called cabosses, directly on its trunk or branches. When they are harvested, the cabosses are split open with machetes to gather the beans, which are white at this point. The beans are washed, fermented by immersion, and then dried before being exported. Pyrogenic/cocoa bitter, powdery, milky, liqueur like

raw_material: Coconut Oil Fractionated (Coconut MCT) Fixed Oil
description:
Coconut Oil Fractionated (Coconut MCT) is a stable almost odourless but with a slightly sweet oily coconut character, carrier oil. It has a very light texture with fast absorption into the skin. Ideal for mixing with other heavier carrier oils or blends (10-50%) to lighten skin feel and aid quicker absorption. Produces an even lighter effect when mixed 50/50 with iso-Propyl Myristate (IPM).
Can be used as a solvent in perfumery compounds where its sweet natural coconut oilyness can be utilised as a blender in sweet and fruity fragrances but due to poor solubility with ethanol, water, resins, citrus oils (terpenes), powders and crystals make sure to test well first.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Coconut Oil Fractionated (Coconut MCT) Fixed Oil

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=>  Very faint warm-oily odour with a very slightly sweet oily natural Coconut character
Perfume-Uses=>  
 Primarily were a natural food grade solvent is required. The slight coconut note may be useful in fruity, sweet and edible types where this will add a natural blending effect.

description: Decatone (Givaudan) has a Citrus, Grapefruit, Woody, Fruity, Fresh odour.Decatone has a woody, vetiver, citrus-grapefruit and green-rhubarb character with a woody vetiver backgrround.It can be used in simple citrus accords where it produces contrast and fixation, and in sophisticated perfumes where it is an excellent blender for oakmoss, vetiver, sandalwood and quinolines. It is powerful and long lasting.

odour_raw:
The odour and uses of DEWBERRY F-TEC 

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=>dewberry, fruit, green, slight dairy character, vanilla, iris, konifer, narcotic, very faint iceberg  

 Synaesthesia=> Morning dampness on a green-leafed stem full of dark berries 

 Perfume-Uses=> Modifier, a nice top note, in the style of many younger men’s and women’s designer fragrances 

  Tips=>

 Just-try-it=>
synonyms: $FL : DEWBERRY F-TEC™ is a primary odour perfume compound base, a mixture of aroma chemicals, isolates and essential oils for use in perfume compounding. Great for quickly adding special effects, rapid prototyping and perfume creation with easier IFRA & EU compliance. When combined with other F-TEC bases, as in the F-TEC Custom Creation Kit, it forms a powerful super-fast and flexible system of perfume creation for most fine perfumery and toiletry product applications.  Q. What are Fleuressence and F-TEC Bases?  "Fleuressence vs F-TEC"

description:
Diacetyl is strong butter sweet creamy pungent caramel buttery fatty-oily vanilla cream - very powerful diffusive top-note-modifier jasmine cypress lavender citronella champaca for-diffusion orris/iris fir-conifer.
BEST USED IN 1% DILUTION OR LESS!
100% not recommended for clients unfamiliar with this material. Can cause lung damage ("Popcorn Lung") if heated in poorly ventilated areas.

description:
Diethyl Phthalate (DEP) is a virtually odourless aromatic solvent. Whilst being a very efficient solvent it is also quite toxic. It is NOT restricted by IFRA. If used best use for non-skin contact products. It tends to depress perfume notes. We supply this for training and experimental purposes only. We do not supply in bulk.
Consider replacing with Triethyl Citrate (TEC) or other lower toxicity solvents like DPG,IPM,BB,BA.

 Solvent best for use with non-skin products depresses odour Plasticiser-avoid-plastic contact

description: Dipropylene Glycol (DPG) is virtually odourless with a slightly-ethereal-green solvent note. Interesting as a versatile solvent as it mixes with most essential oils but is also water soluble. Acts as stabilizer for compounds due to its viscosity when used around 30% in a compound. Not recommended for excessive use in compounds for cold-process soaps where over 5% can start to slow or inhibit tracing/saponification. Avoid in oily or wax based products such as candles, wax melts, massage oils, lipsticks etc. due to solubility problems.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Dipropylene Glycol (DPG)

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=>  odourless slight-ethereal-green virtually odourless, slightly aromatic
Perfume-Uses=>  solvent reasonable tenacity, moderately alkali-stable as a fond for floral, jasmin-like, perfumes for detergents & cosmetics

synonyms: $EG : E-EDIBLE F-TEC™ is a primary odour perfume compound base, a mixture of aroma chemicals, isolates and essential oils for use in perfume compounding. Great for quickly adding special effects, rapid prototyping and perfume creation with easier IFRA & EU compliance. When combined with other F-TEC bases, as in the F-TEC Custom Creation Kit, it forms a powerful super-fast and flexible system of perfume creation for most fine perfumery and toiletry product applications. Perhaps one of the sweetest woody raw materials. It is diffusive and lends depth to a fragrance | Synaesthesia=> Breakfast with a maple syrup pancake. In some far away fairytale, or alternate universe, they eat wooden pancakes; this is what they use as syrup | Perfume-Uses=> For, muguet, chypre, fixative, carnation, modern-green-florals, jasmine | Just-try-it=> | Tips=>

description: Elemi Gum is fresh terpene lemon peppery citrus dill woody balsamic eau-de-colognes fresh-topnotes.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Elemi Gum

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=>  herbal fresh terpene peppery lemony woody balsam dill: Balsam Elemi Use: Manila elemi is the oleoresin which exudes from the bark of Canarium trees when they are tapped or wounded. The essential oil, distilled from Manila Elemi, is mainly used for fragrance applications (such as soap and perfumes) and as a base for liniment. Manila Elemi still finds occasional use as an ingredient in lacquers and varnishes, where it gives toughness and elasticity to the dried film.
Perfume-Uses=>  balsam bergamot

description: Ethyl Safranate has a fresh, edible, natural, herbal damascone rose character. It also has apple, black prune, woody, and saffron qualities.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Ethyl Safranate

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=>An intensive and diffusive, saffron, natural, fresh, damascone, rose, apple, prune with woody aspects. It has great longevity.

Synaesthesia=>

Perfume-Uses=> Ethyl Safranate has good tenacity, reasonable alkali and odour stability, but poor in liquid bleach, acidic cleaner liquid, after shave, and cold wave lotion; however, it is good in other applications.

Just-try-it=>

Tips=>We recommend using Saffranal as a 10% or 1% dilution due to its high impact.

description: Farnesol is mild light-woody  linden floral angelica blender-floralizer in all florals.
odour_raw: The odour and uses of Farnesol | Odour=> Farnesol is a delicate, fresh green floral. It has a clean muguet and linalool quality. It has a sweet-oily odor that develops slowly. Essentially, farnesol is a floral fresh-green note reminiscent of certain notes from Muguet. The tenacity is outstanding but the material as such tends to develop a different odor if it is exposed to air for longer time (blotter test). odour: Farnesol is a delicate, fresh green floral. It has a clean muguet and linalool quality. It has a sweet-oily odor that develops slowly. Essentially, farnesol is a floral fresh-green note reminiscent of certain notes from Muguet. The tenacity is outstanding but the material as such tends to develop a different odor if it is exposed to air for longer time (blotter test). Other descriptors: Delicate Fresh Green Odor With a Muguet Note

description: (Reported as Female pheromones primarily attractive to Males, lesbian-partners)*Not available in Thailand
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Female Concentrate

                                          
                                          
                                          Using Copulins 1000 ppm Concentrate. 
We recommended to using Copulins 1000 ppm in fragrance compounds at a maximum of 0.01 to 0.1% (w/w) 0.1 to 1 gram per Kg or Litre of perfume compound. This will ensure that the active Copulins content does not exceed 1 ppm 1 part-per-million which has been found to be the upper limit at which the pheromones are esthetically acceptable.
The materials used are high purity reconstructions using synthetic sources and no human or animal extracts were used in their production.
The Copulins are a combination of 6 types of highly purified volatile fatty acids found in vaginal secretions in female humans and mammals. They increase in quantity prior to ovulation and are believed to signal readiness. However, as human ovulation is concealed it is thought that they may be used for reasons other than sexual communication and have been linked to female long term bonding in relationships.
Source Wikipedia.

description: Frangipani F-TEC has an amazing almost transcendental odour, a white balsamic floral in the Orchid odour family with heady Cananga & Bourbon Vanilla.
odour_raw: The odour and uses of FRANGIPANI F-TEC | Odour=> Frangipanni F-TEC has an amazing almost transcendental odour, a white balsamic floral in the Orchid odour family with heady Cananga & Bourbon Vanilla. | Synaesthesia Responses=>Soft, cotton-wool fluffy, rich creamy white, waxy petal, romantic, freshly laundered white sheets undulating gently in a warm breeze on a sunny late afternoon, white cascade, the mellow swoosh of crystalline water, pure white fluffy clouds | Perfume-Uses=>Can be used as it is. As a heart note in White Floral bouquets. As a blender – floralizer in Gourmandes, Arabic Orientals, Oud, Aldehydic Florals, | Tips=>Works well with Coconut, Peach, Sandalwood, Pear, Mango, Watermelon, Blackcurrant, Rose Damascena, Neroli, Ozone, Osmanthus, Cherry and ALL White Florals | Just-try-it=>with  Lemon F-TEC or Lemon essential oil as an accord.
perfume_uses: Can be used as it is. As a heart note in White Floral bouquets. As a blender – floralizer in Gourmandes, Arabic Orientals, Oud, Aldehydic Florals,
tips: Works well with Coconut, Peach, Sandalwood, Pear, Mango, Watermelon, Blackcurrant, Rose Damascena, Neroli, Ozone, Osmanthus, Cherry and ALL White Florals

synonyms: $IF : FRUITY IONONE F-TEC™ is a primary odour perfume compound base, a mixture of aroma chemicals, isolates and essential oils for use in perfume compounding. Great for quickly adding special effects, rapid prototyping and perfume creation with easier IFRA & EU compliance. When combined with other F-TEC bases, as in the F-TEC Custom Creation Kit, it forms a powerful super-fast and flexible system of perfume creation for most fine perfumery and toiletry product applications.  Q. What are Fleuressence and F-TEC Bases?  "Fleuressence vs F-TEC"

description: Galaxolide is strong* diffusive sweet floral musky, clean macrocyclic soap-powder-like lactonic slightly fruity Perfume Uses:  popular fixative in all types, all-florals especially muguet laundry soap.*Note: About 30% of people are anosmic to Galaxolide (ie. cannot consciously smell it).  Undiluted it is very viscous and tends to polymerise - recommended to use at 50% if unfamiliar with this material.

                                          Odour=>  strong diffusive sweet floral musk powerful clean macrocylic musk Sweet and musky odor of good tenacity comparable to that of many related products but much inferior to the tenacity of the modern macrocyclic Lactone type Musks

 Pear, Rice,

description: Geranium Essential Oil is bright and fresh with a green-leaf, minty rose body. This delicious essential oil has perfect touches of sweet rose and light carnation
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Geranium Essential Oil | Odour=> Geranium essential oil has many gorgeous features which Mother Nature herself perfectly balanced: a sweet fresh geranium, fresh white rose with pleasant sweetness, fatty, metallic, faint sulphurous, rosy-sweet, muguet, carnation, wallflower | Synaesthesia=> Every fragrant experience has a story, and this one speaks of nascent love. A hooded knight clops down a moor. 
The sunrise makes the very air appear golden-rose. A light coral hue shimmers off his breastplate. In one hand he holds a morning star, in the other he raises a blood-pink flower. 
His love runs across the field to meet him. Some heather sways in the grass as their silhouettes merge on its canvas, as if painted. He lifts her onto his stead and they gallop over the rolling field into the morning sun. other key words: purple color, heartthrob, romance, sensual, garden, sweetheart, Valentine’s Day, a novel about love | Tips=> | Perfume-Uses=> A preferred essential oil in perfumery work, often used to aid rose accords or as a rose substitute; other uses in: Muguet, Carnation, Wallflower
Mint Turkish-delight (Rose oil) | Blends-well-with => lavender, patchouli, clove, rose, orange blossom, sandalwood, jasmine, juniper, bergamot and other citrus oils.

The sunrise makes the very air appear golden-rose. A light coral hue shimmers off his breastplate. In one hand he holds a morning star, in the other he raises a blood-pink flower. 
His love runs across the field to meet him. Some heather sways in the grass as their silhouettes merge on its canvas, as if painted. He lifts her onto his stead and they gallop over the rolling field into the morning sun. other key words: purple color, heartthrob, romance, sensual, garden, sweetheart, Valentine’s Day, a novel about love

description:
Grapeseed Oil has an oily bland odour it is used for massage oil bases. Occasionally used	as a diluent for natural perfumery, but please note you should include an antioxidant such as Rosemary Extract to slow down rancidity.

Not really recommended as a solvent in perfumery compounds due to poor solubility of resins, citrus oils (terpenes), powders and crystals plus will tend to produce rancid notes.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Grapeseed Oil - Fixed Oil

                                          
                                          
                                          Oily bland fatty odour good skin feel and absorbs well. Mainly used for massage oils in Aromatherapy. Not really used in general perfumery.

description: HELVETOLIDE®(Firmenich) offers excellent volatile pear-like facets and smooth ambrette undertones. Its unexpected volume and richness propelled it as a cornerstone of perfumery ingredients. It fixates without suppressing a fragrance, it can skew floral notes and smooths fruity notes without functional connotations.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Helvetolide

                                          
                                          
                                          musk ambrette fruity pear: : a sophisticated, modern musky note with a fruity pear aspect. olfactively, between ambrettolide and ethylene brassylate use:: this musk performs particularly well in liquid applications (alcohol solution, shampoo, body wash) and emulsions. it is one of the few top note musks. A sophisticated, modern musky note with a fruity pear aspect. Olfactively, between Ambrettolide and Ethylene Brassylate  musk not found in nature :

description: Hydratropic Aldehyde is a fresh and sharp green hyacinth leaf
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Hydratropic Aldehyde

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=> Very powerful and penetrating, green-earthy, foliage-like odor in extreme dilution becoming quite floral, Lilac-Hyacinth-like, and slightly fruity. Pleasantly free from the stinging pungency of Phenylacetaldehyde. At dilutions below 1 ppm, the taste is rather sweet-earthy-green and somewhat fruity, depending on accompanying acidity.

Synaesthesia=>
Color: Deep green with hints of lilac
Sound: Rustling of leaves in a garden
Texture: Smooth and velvety like a flower petal
Taste: Sweet and slightly tangy like a ripe fruit
Feeling: Fresh and invigorating green
Image: A vibrant garden filled with blooming flowers

Perfume-Uses=>
Hydratropic Aldehyde is frequently used in floral perfumes, especially in Hyacinth and Lilac where it may outperform Phenylacetaldehyde. It is also used in Rose, Narcissus, Tulip, and many exotic and tropical floral types, as well as certain non-floral fragrances such as transparent linear fragrances.

Therapeutic Uses=>
This material is not commonly used in aromatherapy or aromachology.

Tips=>
Hydratropic Aldehyde is a very powerful aroma chemical, so use it sparingly to avoid overpowering the perfume composition. It works well in floral and fruity fragrances and can add a unique green and earthy aspect to the blend.

Just-try-it=>
Imaginative use for Hydratropic Aldehyde: Create a refreshing spring-inspired perfume by combining this material with green leafy notes, lilac, and a touch of fruity accord. Experiment with different concentrations to find the right balance between green-earthy and floral-fruity elements.

description:
Indolene ND Replacer is a low discolouring option where an Indol or Indolene note is required but discolouration needs to be avoided. It has a strong narcotic floral moth ball naphthelene erogenic-floral animallic jasmine orange-flower odour but is less fecal than indole or indolene. 
 Especially useful in  White flower fragrances, including Jasmine, Orange Flower, Neroli, Tuberose, Lily of the Valley (Muguet), Gardenia,  Narcissus, Jonquille, Cassie and Chypre, Oriental, Coffee, Arabic types.

 All types where the richness of Indolene or Indol is required with far less discolouration issues.

description:
Isobutavan has a sweet and creamy vanillic character reminiscent of white chocolate, cream soda with a soft apricot feeling.
(Previous SKU was 4VV14347)
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Isobutavan | Odour=>sweet vanilla, faint milk, creamy fruity, caramel, icing or ice cream, chocolate: Heavy, sweet, vanilla, nutmeg, Sweet, Fruity, vanillic, hint of sour milk. | Synaesthesia=> pie, whipped cream, baby bottle | Perfume-Uses=>Isobutavan is much less discolourising than vanillin or ethyl vanillin and can be used at levels up to 2pc in for example white soap fragrances before discolouration is a problem. It is less powdery than vanillin and has a creamier thickening effect.  Can be used in fragrances, especially in white soap applications where discoloration might occur | Just-try-it=>use in gourmand fragrances, fine perfumery | Tips=>use to evoke or enhance the following aromas, balsam butterscotch chocolate cream toffee vanilla
caramel cream soda; not found in nature
synaesthesia: pie, whipped cream, baby bottle
perfume_uses: Isobutavan is much less discolourising than vanillin or ethyl vanillin and can be used at levels up to 2pc in for example white soap fragrances before discolouration is a problem. It is less powdery than vanillin and has a creamier thickening effect.  Can be used in fragrances, especially in white soap applications where discoloration might occur
tips:
use to evoke or enhance the following aromas, balsam butterscotch chocolate cream toffee vanilla
caramel cream soda; not found in nature

description: Isopropyl Myristate (IPM) is a virtually odourless solvent. Used as a cosmetic emollient and solvent to replace ethanol in non-alcoholic skin-perfumes. Poor solubility with terpenes, crystals and resinoids but Ok if they are first blended with other materials. Used as a solvent in perfumes for oil or wax products like candles, wax melts, massage oils, hair oil etc.

synonyms: $JN : J-JASMINE F-TEC™ is a primary odour perfume compound base, a mixture of aroma chemicals, isolates and essential oils for use in perfume compounding. Great for quickly adding special effects, rapid prototyping and perfume creation with easier IFRA & EU compliance. When combined with other F-TEC bases, as in the F-TEC Custom Creation Kit, it forms a powerful super-fast and flexible system of perfume creation for most fine perfumery and toiletry product applications. description: Javanol has an intense sandalwood woody oily character. Useful for chypre carnation modern-green-florals orientals Indian bouquets. Blends well with modern Agarwood and Oud fragrances.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Javanol

                                          
                                          
                                          tropical woody fatty sandalwood herbal cologne floral: Sandalwood, Creamy, Rosy, Powerful Use: Javanol is a new-generation sandalwood molecule with unprecedented power and substantivity. It has a rich, natural, creamy sandalwood note like beta santanol. With its exceptonal low threshold, it is approximately 8x more effective in wash tests on a weight basis than the most powerful known product. In addition, its very good stability, due in part to the complete absence of double bond, makes it suitable for all applications except bleach. description:
Jojoba Oil is a bland smelling oily carrier oil. It has a fairly viscous texture with moderate absorption into the skin leaving a protective film. Ideal for mixing with other lighter carrier oils or blends (10-30%) to increase richness to aid a prolonged massage without constant re-application. 
Not really recommended as a solvent in perfumery compounds due to poor solubility of resins, citrus oils (terpenes), powders and crystals.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Jojoba Oil - Fixed Oil

                                          
                                          
                                          Bland oily odour. Used as a carrier for essential oils or as a massage oil. Oily texture and quite slow absorbtion into the skin.

description: A fresh lightly woody floral with faint lavender touches
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of L-Linalool Fleuressence

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=> Light chemical floral Linalool Bergamot Rosewood Lavender Coriander

 Synaesthesia=> light grey, silver,  earl grey tea, citrus leaf, cymbal, modern, crisp,

 Perfume-Uses=> a widely used blender in all fragrances types, fresh floral, woody, petitgrain, bois de rose, bergamot Muguet Lavender Bergamot All-types, Lily, Peony, Freesia, Sweet-pea, Frangipani, Honeysuckle, Orange-flower, Apple blossom, Lemon Lime, Grape, Cola, lemon lime soft drinks, Blackcurrant, Apricot, Pineapple, Date, Banana, Peach, Plum, Spice, Chocolate

Tips=>use carefully with green, spicy, phenolic or sharp notes as it may unexpectedly boost the chemical character of them if overdone 

 Just-try-it=> in any floral with Hedione for a petal effect
synonyms: $LL : L-Linalool Fleuressence™ is a primary odour perfume compound base, a mixture of aroma chemicals, isolates and essential oils for use in perfume compounding. Used with other aroma materials and essential oils it is great for quickly adding special effects, rapid prototyping and perfume creation. When combined with other Fleuressence bases, as in the Fleuressence Custom Creation Kit, it forms a part of a powerful super-fast and flexible system of perfume creation for most fine perfumery applications. description: Lilial Substitute IFRA has a fresh light green floral lily, lily-of-the-valley,  muguet lindenblossom aldehyde lilac modern odour. Lilial Substitute IFRA is IFRA-49 and EU compliant and can be used to replace Lilial in almost all applications where Lilial would be used. It has a fresh soapy clean floral odour delicately sweet-light-floral with excellent-tenacity and radiation, lasting 168+ hours on a smelling strip. Useful in all floral, flowery perfumes as a floralizer and fixative and can generally be used without limitation at normally used levels in perfumes, toiletries and cosmetics products.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Lilial Substitute IFRA

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=>  fresh light green soapy floral lily notes muguet lindenblossom
Perfume-Uses=>  Muguet Aldehyde Lilac Lindenblossom Modern
 No FEMA Number

description: Linalyl Propionate is fresh bergamot lily woody rose rum.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Linalyl Propionate

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=> Linalyl propionate has a fresh, floral, light chemical but slightly sickly-sweet aroma. Lavender-like. 

Synaesthesia=> The floral and fresh aroma may evoke feelings of spring and summer. 

Perfume-Uses=> Linalyl propionate is most used in small amounts as a Linalyl Acetate modifier. If overdone it can smell sickly 

Tips=>  Linalyl propionate blends well with other fresh floral notes such as lavender, geranium, and bergamot. 

Just-try-it=>  In Eau-de-Colognes to replace or supplement Linalyl Acetate.

description: (Reported as a Male pheromone primarily attractive to females) *Not available in Thailand
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Male Concentrate

                                          
                                          
                                          Using Androsterone 1000 ppm Concentrate
We recommended to using Androsterone 1000 ppm in fragrance compounds at a maximum of 0.01 to 0.1% (w/w) 0.1 to 1 gram per Kg or Litre of perfume compound. This will ensure that the active Androsterone content does not exceed 1 ppm 1 part-per-million which has been found to be the upper limit at which the pheromones are esthetically acceptable in odour.
The materials used are high purity reconstructions using synthetic sources and no human or animal extracts were used in their production.
Androsterone is found in the human axilla and skin as well as in urine. It may also be secreted by human sebaceous glands. Androsterone has been shown to naturally occur in pine pollen and is well known in many animal species. Androsterone has been found to affect human behavior when smelled.
Source Wikipedia.

odour_raw: The odour and uses of MALE WOODSY F-TEC | Odour=>The heart of many woody masculine perfumes, with a fresh light cool crisp effervescent citrus top note blended beautifully with classy woody notes of mature cedar and vetivert | Synaesthesia=>Yellow amber tan light-brown, beige. Woodwind instruments, flute, clarinet, tamborine. Baritone. Earth, Wind, knock-on-wood bamboo, reeds, bitter, grainy, sand, relaxed, classy, youthful mature male, | Perfume-Uses=>Use as the centre piece of male woody perfumes, Easily transformed in any direction by accentuating with other specifc woods especially Vetiver, Patchouli, Cedarwood and for an Arabic flavour with Agarwood. An excellent accompaniment as a blender modifier for Leather, Chypre and Suede perfumes. | Tips=>A better performing version of Male Woodsy Fleuressence. Use 25-75% of an adult male fragrance or assertive female fragrance | Just-try-it=>This is the secret ingredient to give your animalic perfumes, Chypre, Leather etc. that high-class designer finish. From just 1% in the perfume compound will add the Je ne sais quoi, that indefinable, elusive quality, that charms everybody
synaesthesia: Yellow amber tan light-brown, beige. Woodwind instruments, flute, clarinet, tamborine. Baritone. Earth, Wind, knock-on-wood bamboo, reeds, bitter, grainy, sand, relaxed, classy, youthful mature male,
perfume_uses: Use as the centre piece of male woody perfumes, Easily transformed in any direction by accentuating with other specifc woods especially Vetiver, Patchouli, Cedarwood and for an Arabic flavour with Agarwood. description:
Manzanate has a fruity green apple pineapple melon odour
Its fruity ripe apple character has aspects of cider and sweet pineapple. When used in perfumes for water-contact products it imparts a good impact and bloom. In fine fragrances its lends a modern fruity sparkle to the top notes.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Manzanate

                                          
                                          
                                          fresh fruity green melon apple skin pineapple natural waxy: : A fruity, pineapple odor with natural connotations Use:: For a sweet powerful pineapple note in fragrances. Flavor:: green Used in pineapple and apple flavors. Can also be used in a tutti-frutti composition. Fruity, Apple, Pineapple Use: Manzanate has a fruity apple character with aspects of cider and sweet pineapple. It works very well at covering base odours and has great impact in water-contact products. Fresh, fruity-green, reminiscent of apple, unripe plum peel and pineapple Use: This ester has wide usage as a top note in green apple compositions, especially for shampoos, but also for foam baths, air-fresheners, cleaners and toiletries. high impact fresh, fruity-green note, evocative of apple skins pineapple note, slightly minty Flavor: cooling, green, cucumber, tropical & oily : pineapple Fruity, Apple, Pineapple  apple cucumber fresh & clean green herbal lily melon honeydew melon watermelon muskmelon cantaloupe musk neroli pineapple topn

description:
Mate Absolute has a lovely natural Matcha-Green-Tea  odour. Useful in herbal, fougere, forest and tobacco  type fragrances or wherever a natural Green Tea effect is required. 
Please note it is a dark green solid requiring heating to dissolve.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Mate Absolute

                                          
                                          
                                          Matcha Green Tea  herbal leafy and hay slight phenolic with tobacco nuances
Perfume Uses=> Fougere Green Tea Tobacco Herbal Moss Fern
Notes on Use=> Mate Absolute is a dark green solid. It is a difficult material to dissolve, requiring heating and will throw out of products containing high terpene content essential oils (e.g, Orange, Pine etc), fixed carrier oils or cosmetic oils (eg. IPM, Mineral Oil, Cyclomethicone). It usually needs to be incorporated lower than 5% in a compound of low terpene essential oils and/or aroma chemicals. Solvents that seem to work best are Ethanol (99.80%), DPG, Benzyl Benzoate, Benzyl Alcohol.

description: MAYOL is Fresh soft and clean floral odour associated with the white petals and blossoms
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Mayol

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=> fresh clean floral magnolia hydroxycitronellal cumin grassy: : Soft,clean white floral odor reminiscent of muguet and magnolia Use:: Can be used in all floral creations for fine fragrance and skin care. : Fresh soft and clean floral odour that recalls the fragrance associated with the white petals and blossoms of many flowers, magnolia, tuberose, muguet, etc. Use:: MAYOL ® harmonizes wonderfully with muguet, lilac, tuberose, jasmin floral and with citrus notes particularly bergamot. It combines well with muguet ingredients, often giving lift and smoothness to the fragrance. With woody and methyl ionone blends, MAYOL ® diffuses the woody character to the top of the perfume. Small percentages give floral freshness and large amounts can produce original effects. : Floral, Muguet Use:: Floral, muguet. Useful where a stable muguet note is required. Fresh soft and clean floral odour that recalls the fragrance associated with the white petals and blossoms of many flowers, magnolia, tuberose, muguet, etc. Perfume-Uses=>  a

description: Methyl Diantilis is an elegant, spicy sweet ingredient with a similar olfactive note to Isoeugenol.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Methyl Diantilis

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=> spicy carnation sweet vanilla : Spicy, Carnation, Sweet, Vanilla Use: Methyl Diantilis, a Givaudan captive for several years, is an elegant, spicy/sweet ingredient with a similar olfactive note to Isoeugenol, but with an additional powdery aspect reminiscent of carnation (Dianthus caryophyllus). Unlike Isoeugenol, it is much less susceptible to discolouration and, importantly, is not restricted by IFRA. Spicy, Carnation, Sweet, Vanilla Perfume-Uses=>  carnation dianthus oeillet clover trefle le'trefle incarnat floral spice not found in nature :

description: METHYL LAITONE 10% DPG  is extremely powerful and very effective in all accords where a creamy, fruity volume is required.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of METHYL LAITONE 10% DPG

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=> lactonic coconut meadow tropical creamy dairy plum: Lactonic, Creamy, Coconut Milk, Coumarin Use: Methyl Laitone 10pc is one of the new spiro -lactones developed and patented by Givaudan. It is extremely powerful and very effective in all accords where a creamy, fruity volume is required. It provides cosmetic body to white flower notes such as jasmine, gardenia, tuberose and ylang ylang, and to fruity accords like peach and osmanthus. In sandalwood accords it provides milkiness. Coupling with coumarin and hay notes makes it extremely interesting and polyvalent. Its dry note is very noticeable, especially on humid and dry fabric. Lactonic, Creamy, Coconut Milk, Coumarin Perfume-Uses=>  amber apricot balsam banana bay bay rum bouquet caramel cedarwood chocolate cocoa chypre coconut coconut tropical femme fern fougere fig floral fracas fruit tropical fruit gardenia grass sweet hay new mown hay foin coupe honeysuckle chevrefeuille jasmin juniper berry lavender lilac lilas syringa lychee mace magnolia maple nutmeg orange orch

description: Methyl salicylate, is a colorless liquid with a strong, wintergreen, sweet birch, and minty aroma. It is found  the leaves of the wintergreen plant (Gaultheria procumbens) and is also found in the oil of birch and some other plants. It is commonly used as a flavoring and fragrance ingredient in a wide variety of products, such as candies, mints, mouthwash, and topical pain relievers. It is also used in perfumes and toiletries, as well as in the production of salicylic acid, which is used in making aspirin and other medications.

description:
Musk Ketone has a quite natural musk character dry powdery sweet and very tenacious. The only remaining nitro musk that has not yet been prohibited by IFRA restrictions. If you wish to reconstruct many classic landmark fragrances this is a must in your palette of materials. 
Keywords: floral-fruity sweet tenacious musky discrete animal amber oriental aldehydic-floral leather chypre fixative soap gardenia mimosa abronea.

description:
Odour=> Musk X Super is a synergistic nitro musk base with a powerful silky smooth musk character. Contains IFRA compliant Musk Ketone. Odour life on the smelling strip is 300+ hours.


Synaesthesia=> It has a clean, delicate and sensual aroma that evokes images of fresh laundry and skin. 

Perfume-Uses=> Musk X Super can be  widely used in perfumes and other fragrances as a base note, providing a soft and clean background for other aromas. 

Tips=>  Add it too other musk combinations to give warmth 

Just-try-it=>  Use it with elegant fragrances, or to create unique scents.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Musk X Super

description: Nagarmotha Oil has a woody earthy dry spicy cedar Agarwood-like odour. Also known as Cypriol Oil (Cyperus scariosus Oil)  it is sometimes used as a cheaper all-natural replacement for Oud or Agarwood. It is not as deep, rich or animalic as Agarwood so best used with some of the natural oil or with one of our bases. Useful in Chypres, Oriental and Middle Eastern perfumes and blends well with wood, iris, ionones, oakmoss, olibanum, labdanum. (~nargamotha)
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Nagarmotha Oil 

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=>  
Woody, spicy, between patchouli and vetiver, earthy odor with agarwood undertones
Perfume-Uses=>  
Woody Arabian perfumes Blends well with Agarwood (Oudh)  trace modifier in florals and other western style perfumes to give depth.

description:
Narcissus F-TEC has an intense floral-sweet, rich narcotic, slightly phenolic odor.
NARCISSUS F-TEC is primary odour perfume compound base, a mixture of aroma chemicals, isolates and essential oils for perfume compounding.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of NARCISSUS F-TEC 

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour= Narcissus F-TEC has an intense floral-sweet, rich narcotic, slightly phenolic odor.
Synaesthesia responses:
Colours= deep bright yellow, amber reddy-brown
Sounds= A gentle rumble, soft humming, lava flowing
Textures= soft putty, molten gold
Tastes= Sweet honeyed floral, nectar
Feelings= peaceful, serene, deep sleep, trance-like-state, hypnotic
Images= A peaceful meadow of narcisse, jonquille, daffodils, Salvadore Dali surrealistic paintings
Perfume-Uses= Narcissus F-TEC is used in a variety of perfumes, including intense florals, floral-orientals, chypres, and art nouveau classic fragrances. It is also used in some citrus and green scents. Combine it with other floral notes especially white florals, and rich notes such as sandalwood, agarwood and animal notes.
Aromachology-Uses= Narcissus F-TEC has been used in aromachology to help promote feelings of joy, happiness, and contentment. It can also help to reduce feelings of stress and anxiety. But warning, in excess can become overwhelming.
Tips= When using Narcissus F-TEC in a perfume formula, it is important to use it in moderation as it can be quite strong and overpowering.
Just-try-it= Works really well with Blackcurrant and intense fruit notes.

When combined with other F-TEC bases, as in the F-TEC Custom Creation Kit, it forms a powerful super-fast and flexible system of perfume creation for most fine perfumery and toiletry product applications.                                           sweet green waxy violet pear fruity melon vegetable: : intense fresh, green, violet, cucumber Use:: Methyl-2-nonenoate imparts freshness and sparkle to floral fragrances, particularly violet. An excellent substitute for methyl heptine carbonate (folione) or methyl octine carbonate. Flavor:: green Used in baked goods, gelatins, puddings and beverages. Fatty-nutty notes evolving to foliage green : Floral, Fruity, Green, Waxy Use:: Suggested Uses: Apple, Apricot, Dairy Products, Hard Fruits, Herbal Flavours, Peach, Soft Fruits Green, Floral, Fresh, Powerful Use: Neofolione blends very well with most floral notes, especially violet, where it is particularly useful in replacing Folione. description: A very realistic neroli flower blossom, smooth orange with a green quality
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of NEROLI F-TEC 

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=>neroli, orange blossom, floral, narcotic, green feel; minor constituents: Linalool, aliFATic Narcotic, Rose, Muguet, Wood, Zolvent, Light Citrus, Fruit, iceberg, jasmin, Muguet, Animal 

 Synaesthesia=> lovely, addictive, comforting orange flower, in a sunny field, rays of the sun diffused through a tree

 Perfume-Uses=> perfume modifier

  Tips=>A stunning top note, blends well with Lemon EO and other citruses

 Just-try-it=> in the style of Tom Ford Neroli Portofino, Bvlgari Wood Neroli, Aqua di Parma Essenzia
synonyms: $CN : NEROLI F-TEC™ is a primary odour perfume compound base, a mixture of aroma chemicals, isolates and essential oils for use in perfume compounding. Great for quickly adding special effects, rapid prototyping and perfume creation with easier IFRA & EU compliance. When combined with other F-TEC bases, as in the F-TEC Custom Creation Kit, it forms a powerful super-fast and flexible system of perfume creation for most fine perfumery and toiletry product applications. description:
Odour=> Neryl Acetate has a fresh floral, citrus white rose-like aroma. 

Synaesthesia=> Neryl Acetate can evoke feelings of fresh warmth and comfort. 

Perfume-Uses=> Neryl Acetate is commonly used in floral perfumes to add a fresh rosy note. 

Tips=>  Neryl Acetate is normally only used in small amounts. 

Just-try-it=>  Try blending Neryl Acetate with other floral ingredients like jasmine or ylang-ylang for a unique and complex floral scent.

                                          floral rose soapy citrus dewy pear: Orange-Blossom, Rose : A sweet floral, orange flower, citrus, fresh rose odor with a tropical undertone Use:: Used in neroli, jasmine, peony and magnolia compositions. description:
Incredibly powerful diffusive dry woody amber note of outstanding tenacity. Probably one of the most powerful Amber notes available. A complex and diffusive woody amber note that gives vibrant effects to the complete olfactive pyramid from top to base. Beaming with elegant musky notes, it offers the creamy warmth of Amber. 
(+)-(3R)-1-[(1R,6S)-2,2,6-Trimethylcyclohexyl]-3-hexanol
Whilst listed as a synonym of Nimberol, Timberol it is 50-100 times more powerful!
Purest key olfactive isomer available on the market, NORLIMBANOL® DEXTRO from Firmenich is a breakthrough innovative molecule.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Norlimbanol Dextro

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=>  diffusive, woody, with aspects of sandalwood
Perfume-Uses=>  excellent tenacity, stable in all media woody component for functional perfumery

description:
A Givaudan Speciality,  
Nympheal™ is an outstanding fresh and opulent Muguet (Lily of the Valley) ingredient, with a Linden Blossom accent bringing tremendous volume and creaminess.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Nympheal

                                          
                                          
                                          Nympheal™ is a diffusive floral cyclamen muguet note with green, watery and linden blossom facets. Nympheal™ imparts white floral watery density and brings a floral creaminess to the composition and high diffusivity.

Perfumery tips -Synergies

70% – Nympheal™ + 30% – Patchouli Oil
The synergy of Nympheal™ and Patchouli Oil creates a particular and unexpected effect. They do not overlay, but interact to bring a unique signature with a stellar performance.

description: Olibanum Essential Oil is a complex, balsamic frankincense oil derived from the Boswellia sacra—commonly known as the frankincense or olibanum-tree
odour_raw: The odour and uses of Olibanum Essential Oil | Odour=> Olibanum opens with a strong but pleasant citrus. As it begins to develop, the resinous, almost buttery-warm wood quality emerges. It is sweet, lemon woody, and coniferous, but it can be considered the most refined coniferous resin (as some coniferous oils can be sharp and remind people of cleaning products—this is not at all the case with rich olibanum essential oil).  Olibanum essential oil has a rich, resinous sweetness, not the kind of sweetness of candy floss or sugary. Other descriptors: woody, fresh, faint spicy, sweet, resin, balsam | Synaesthesia=>religious ceremony, applying rich and expensive oils to Jesus’s feet | Perfume-Uses=>For citrus-piney, olibanum, frankincense, old wood, fresh woody, terpeney, warm sweet, oriental leather, chypre, mens cistus, incense, citrus verbena, verveine, spicy | Just-try-it=>In the style of some Amouage fragrances; Epic Man, Memoir Man | Tips=>
synaesthesia: religious ceremony, applying rich and expensive oils to Jesus’s feet
perfume_uses: For citrus-piney, olibanum, frankincense, old wood, fresh woody, terpeney, warm sweet, oriental leather, chypre, mens cistus, incense, citrus verbena, verveine, spicy

description:
Olibanum Resinoid Absolute (Solid) is a premium alcohol soluble extract of Frankincense. It has lovely fresh balsamic frankincense odour, citrus-piney fresh terpeney warm sweet leather ideal as a versatile fixative and particularly useful in oriental, chypre, and masculine fragrances.
 Note: This material is solid and is ground to aid handling but may require a good deal of warming to dissolve in your compound so we recommend this material only for experienced users. Alternatively consider Olibanum Resinoid (Viscous) 2QK21856.

description:
Olive Oil is bland oily carrier oil for use in  essential oils massage light texture quick absorption into skin bases year shelf life.
Olive Oil is a carrier oil used in massage oil blends. It has an light oily texture with a quite quick absorption rate into the skin. Add a just enough to a too-slow absorbing blend (e.g 5-10%) to give you the desired absorption rate. 
Not really recommended as a solvent in perfumery compounds due to poor solubility of resins, citrus oils (terpenes), powders and crystals plus will tend to produce rancid notes.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Olive Oil - Fixed Oil

                                          
                                          
                                          Bland oily odour. Used as a carrier for essential oils or as a massage oil. Light oily texture and quick absorbtion into the skin. Deveops a rancid note quickly if not stored correctly. Good for hair products.

raw_material: Palm Oil Fractionated - Fixed Oil (Palm MCT)
description:
Palm Oil Fractionated (certified from renewable sources) is a stable almost odourless, carrier oil. It has a very light texture with fast absorption into the skin. Ideal for mixing with other heavier carrier oils or blends (10-50%) to lighten skin feel and aid quicker absorption. Produces an even lighter effect when mixed 50/50 with iso-Propyl Myristate (IPM).
Can be used as a solvent in perfumery compounds but due to poor solubility of resins, citrus oils (terpenes), powders and crystals make sure to test well first. Note: We have found it tends to produce sour notes over time with some resinoids and wood oils.

Fresh, citrus green cassis blackcurrant passion-fruit

description:
Peru Balsam Resinoid is a sweet balsamic resin with warm touches of vanilla. 
Peru Balsam balsam is a resinoid obtained from Myroxylon balsamum tree, which is native to Central and South America. It is a thick, sticky resin that has a warm, balsamic, vanilla-like aroma with a hint of spice and a slight smoky note.
(contains Benzyl Benzoate from source for handling).

description: A vibrant, fresh, and leafy-green aroma with distinctive tart citrus and floral (orange blossom-like) undertones. It features a sophisticated woody-herbaceous dry-down that provides a natural, rustic elegance. Often referred to as "Poor Man's Neroli," it offers similar floral qualities but with a much greener and more "crunchy" texture.

description:
Dilution of Phenylacetaldehyde 10% in DPG

Odour=> Neat Phenyl Acetaldehyde has a intense and powerful green floral odour reminiscent of Hyacinth, Syringa and Lilac.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of Phenyl acetaldehyde 10% in DPG

                                          
                                          
                                          Odour=>  Dilution of Phenylacetaldehyde 10% in DPG Refer to constituent materials for more information
 Odour=> Neat Phenyl Acetaldehyde has a intense and powerful green floral odour reminscent of Hyacinth, Syringa and Lilac. 

Synaesthesia=> Deep Green, Flowery, Watery, Watercress leaves, Nasturtium, Pond, Monet' Water Lily Pond, Stravinsky's Rite of Spring 

Perfume-Uses=> Phenyl Acetaldehyde is useful to help capture the calyx and leafy notes in all floral types especially Hyacinth, Syringa, Lilac, Carnation (in traces) and Rose 

Tips=>  Phenyl Acetaldehyde is a very powerful and can be quite irritant, so it should be used sparingly in perfumes to avoid overpowering the other ingredients and not becoming irritating. IFRA used to require it to be mixed 50% with PEA (Phenyl Ethyl Alcohol) to quench its irritantcy (this technique is no longer IFRA accepted despite it working).  This 10% dilution in DPG is a good place to start with this material if you are not familiar with it

Just-try-it=>  If you're looking for a green  flowershop fragrance, heavy, humid, intense effect.

description: A very fresh, diffusive, and modern marine-ozonic note with a distinct floral (Muguet/Lily of the Valley) undertone. Its most unique characteristic is a remarkable "cold" or "frozen" effect that adds a shivering freshness to a fragrance without the typical herbal or medicinal scent of menthol.

description:
Pomelo Essential Oil Corsica is a vibrant burst of citrus brilliance, capturing the sun-drenched spirit of the Mediterranean coast. Sparkling with the crisp, tangy clarity of grapefruit and the depth of pomelo, it’s uplifted by delicate Yuzu-like nuances that add an exotic, zesty twist. This oil offers a fresh, effervescent energy that dances on the skin—ideal for perfumers seeking to infuse their creations with a radiant top note that feels both invigorating and refined. Whether anchoring a citrus accord or lifting a complex blend, Corsican Pomelo brings luminous freshness and modern sophistication to any composition. 
<>Pomelo Rectific He Corse - Synarome

description: PWx factor is an odour enhancer, it increases odour life, adds depth, radiance and a maturation effect to perfume compounds and essential oils. Increases substantivity on the skin, paper and cloth. "MSG for perfume". The 13th note that every perfume needs to be complete. The X factor.
odour_raw:
The odour and uses of PWx Factor

                                          
                                          
                                          Almost odourless.
PWX Factor from PerfumersWorld is a fixative that enhances odour. The material is almost odourless by itself, but has a faint fresh solventy character. Relative odour impact is two compared to Linalool (impact 100). It is detectable on a smelling strip for up to 240 hours. The fixative works by adding a layer in the fragrance below the odour threshold without imparting it's own odour; it stimulates a complex maturation effect in a perfume compound without changing the basic odor type or needing time to mature.
                                      
USAGE LEVELS (% OF PERFUME COMPOUND): The general usage recommendation is to add 1% (w/w) into perfume compounds as part of the formulation. For citrus fragrances, it is recommended to limit the use to 0.1-0.5% (w/w). However, up to 5% (w/w) can be used in heavier fragrance types like Orientals, Chypres, Woods, Fougeres and Masculine scents. Overdosing leads to an over-smoothing, or flattening, effect on the perfume. This ingredient is generally stable and non-discoloring in the following product applications: alcoholic perfume, antiperspirants/deo, creams and lotions, talcum powder, tablet soap, liquid soap, shampoo, hair conditioner, bath/shower gel, detergent powder, liquid detergent, fabric softener, candles, potpourri and incense. It is soluble in ethanol, most essential oils, aroma chemicals and compounds. PWx Factor is very slightly soluble in water. 

Interesting Facts: 
* PWX Factor does not contain MSG or animal or human extracts.
* PWX Factor does contain on a fine tuned  GCMS system a lot of DPG and over 700 peaks in the ranges of Parts Per Thousand, Parts per Million, Parts per Billion and Parts Per Trillion. 
* To buy 1 Kg each of the ingredients separately in PWx Factor  would cost you well over US$1Million. 
* When it was first launched in around 2010 it was $6/g and 6 years later, after recovering its setup costs, it dropped to just1 tenth of that price!
* If you do a Workshop with us we explain how it works in detail and demonstrate its power. 
* Generally if it doesn't work for you it has been overdosed - less is more! 
* It's particularly useful if you can't quite get that last nuance in a composition - Just try a drop of the PWx!
synonyms: $XZ : "MSG FOR PERFUME" A non-toxic complex Compounded mixture used as perfume enhancer. (PW-X FACTOR M1 PWX02980): Does not contain MSG or animal products.
